summary: Add the numeric `range {min, max}` predicate over `integer`/`number` fields — parse in `contract.rs`, decide in `engine.rs`, reject min>max in admissibility.

edit.description: Add `Predicate::Range { field, min, max }` (f64 bounds) to the closed algebra; parse a `range = { field, min, max }` clause in `parse_predicate`; reject `min > max` via the inadmissibility path. Covers integer/number fields only.

Decide `Range` in `decide`: pull the field's parsed scalar, fire when it is numeric and falls outside `[min, max]`, hold on absence and on a non-numeric kind (a `type` clause owns that). Add the `min > max` case to `inadmissibilities`. Add unit tests mirroring `max_len_fires_only_past_the_bound`.

tests.asserts: `range` fires only when a numeric field falls outside `[min, max]`, is silent within bounds and on an absent field; a `min > max` range is reported inadmissible.

acceptance: A contract clause `range = { field, min, max }` checks a numeric field stays within `[min, max]` at its declared severity; an inverted range fails admissibility.
notes: The corpus's own narrow named escape for a genuine value bound, explicitly admitted by 45's `Also in scope` after the `type` lattice (`10-contracts.md`) rejected JSON-Schema ranges. Self-contained: a field-scope predicate, no roster or graph machinery — distinct from the roster/model governance predicates (count, membership, degree, acyclic), which await their own entries.

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: embed `contracts/spec.toml`, project `ws.specs` via `spec_features`, and run admissibility + `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds it via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it in a clean commit

edit.description: Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features via `extract::spec_features`, parse the spec contract, and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them.

tests.asserts: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — the over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run still exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: needs SPEC-FEATURES (the extractor, shipped) on disk plus a human to commit the untracked `contracts/spec.toml`. Runs only spec.toml's active `max_lines` clause; its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier — both also downstream of that spec.toml commit.
